# Plan: Populate IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md (Phase 2 Gap Analysis)

## Key Findings from Gap Analysis

### Confirmed Implementation State

| Component | File | State |
|---|---|---|
| ScoreCanvas | `organisms/ScoreCanvas.tsx` | Static React/Tailwind (absolute px coords) — NOT VexFlow |
| useSandboxStore | `store/useSandboxStore.ts` | contextMenu only — no isPlaying, no playhead |
| PlaybackBar | `molecules/PlaybackBar.tsx` | Document-context re-upload bar, not playback controller |
| DropzoneCopy | `organisms/DropzoneCopy.tsx` | Has `accept`, `aria-label`, keyboard nav ✓ — NO error state |
| EnsembleBuilderPanel | `organisms/EnsembleBuilderPanel.tsx` | Local state only — no Zustand EnsembleState slice |
| VoiceDropdown | `molecules/VoiceDropdown.tsx` | POUR-compliant (aria-expanded, role=listbox, keyboard) ✓ |
| StepBar | `molecules/StepBar.tsx` | aria-current="step" ✓ — pending steps NOT non-interactive |
| ThemeToggle | `atoms/ThemeToggle.tsx` | 6 ad-hoc hex violations — aria-label fixed text, missing aria-pressed |
| DocumentHeader | `organisms/DocumentHeader.tsx` | No back nav, no piece title in brand font |
| ScorePreviewPanel | `organisms/ScorePreviewPanel.tsx` | No role="img", no redirect guard |
| DropzoneCopy (doc) | used on doc page | No filename display, no "Change file" link |
| UploadState (Zustand) | MISSING | No slice stores uploaded filename/type/validity |
| EnsembleState (Zustand) | MISSING | No slice stores voice selections |
| ScoreState (Zustand) | MISSING | No notes/durations/beam groups in store |
| usePlayback hook | MISSING | No Tone.js symbolic dispatch |
| RedLineTooltip | MISSING | Sidebar only (ViolationCard), no canvas inline tooltip |
| ScorePaginationDock | MISSING | StepBar ≠ score page dock |
| Score Maximize | MISSING | No expand button anywhere |

### Ambiguities Requiring Superman Decision (Before Task Generation)

**AMBIGUITY-1 — ThemeToggle hex fix (blocked)**
Six ad-hoc hex values in `ThemeToggle.tsx` violate the Nocturne/Sonata token
constraint: `#2D1817`, `#A55B3766`, `#C8B8B6`, `#D2B48C`, `#FFB300`, `#1A1110`, `#4A3B39`.
Per `typescript-patterns.md`, token changes require Superman's explicit approval.
→ **No task generated for hex fix until approval received.**
→ A separate aria-label + aria-pressed task (TASK-A18) is safe and generated independently.

**AMBIGUITY-2 — "Open in Sandbox" vs "Generate Harmonies" CTA**
Spec 003 AC-004 specifies an "Open in Sandbox" primary button. The current
`document/page.tsx` uses `EnsembleBuilderPanel`'s "Generate Harmonies" button
(`onGenerateHarmonies`) to route to `/sandbox`. Are these the same CTA, or should
there be a distinct standalone "Open in Sandbox" button separate from ensemble building?
→ **Needs Superman answer before generating TASK-A16.**

**AMBIGUITY-3 — RedLineTooltip backend JSON shape**
`RedLineTooltip` requires `{ measure, beat, voices, ruleLabel, definition }` from
backend. This shape is specified in spec-001 AC-003 but not yet delivered by a
backend service. Task is generated but marked `[!]` (blocked on backend).

# Implementation Plan

_(This file is generated and maintained by the agent. Do not edit manually during an active loop run.)_

**How to populate:** Run `PROMPT_plan.md` via the Claude CLI or paste it into a planning session. The agent will perform a gap analysis against `specs/` and populate this file with a prioritized task list. Each task includes explicit acceptance criteria derived from the spec.

**How to use:** The `loop.sh` build loop reads this file each iteration. The agent picks the single highest-priority uncompleted task, implements it, validates it (per `AGENTS.md`), marks it complete, and commits.

- `[~]` — In progress (current loop iteration)
- `[x]` — Complete (tests + lint + build passing)
- `[!]` — Blocked (reason noted inline)

     1→---
     2→name: zustand-store-ts
     3→description: "Create Zustand stores with TypeScript, subscribeWithSelector middleware, and proper state/action separation. Use when building React state management, creating global stores, or implementing reacti..."
     4→risk: unknown
     5→source: community
     6→date_added: "2026-02-27"
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Zustand Store
    10→
    11→Create Zustand stores following established patterns with proper TypeScript types and middleware.
    12→
    13→## Quick Start
    14→
    15→Copy the template from assets/template.ts and replace placeholders:
    16→- `{{StoreName}}` → PascalCase store name (e.g., `Project`)
    17→- `{{description}}` → Brief description for JSDoc
    18→
    19→## Always Use subscribeWithSelector
    20→
    21→```typescript
    22→import { create } from 'zustand';
    23→import { subscribeWithSelector } from 'zustand/middleware';
    24→
    25→export const useMyStore = create<MyStore>()(
    26→  subscribeWithSelector((set, get) => ({
    27→    // state and actions
    28→  }))
    29→);
    30→```
    31→
    32→## Separate State and Actions
    33→
    34→```typescript
    35→export interface MyState {
    36→  items: Item[];
    37→  isLoading: boolean;
    38→}
    39→
    40→export interface MyActions {
    41→  addItem: (item: Item) => void;
    42→  loadItems: () => Promise<void>;
    43→}
    44→
    45→export type MyStore = MyState & MyActions;
    46→```
    47→
    48→## Use Individual Selectors
    49→
    50→```typescript
    51→// Good - only re-renders when `items` changes
    52→const items = useMyStore((state) => state.items);
    53→
    54→// Avoid - re-renders on any state change
    55→const { items, isLoading } = useMyStore();
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Subscribe Outside React
    59→
    60→```typescript
    61→useMyStore.subscribe(
    62→  (state) => state.selectedId,
    63→  (selectedId) => console.log('Selected:', selectedId)
    64→);
    65→```
    66→
    67→## Integration Steps
    68→
    69→1. Create store in `src/frontend/src/store/`
    70→2. Export from `src/frontend/src/store/index.ts`
    71→3. Add tests in `src/frontend/src/store/*.test.ts`
    72→
    73→## When to Use
    74→This skill is applicable to execute the workflow or actions described in the overview.
    75→
```
</details></tool-use>

```typescript
import { create } from "zustand";
import { subscribeWithSelector } from "zustand/middleware";
import { persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";

export interface UploadedFile {
  filename: string;
  fileType: string;
  fileValid: boolean;
}

export interface UploadState {
  filename: string | null;
  fileType: string | null;
  fileValid: boolean;
}

export interface UploadActions {
  setUploadedFile: (file: UploadedFile) => void;
  clearUpload: () => void;
}

export type UploadStore = UploadState & UploadActions;

const initialState: UploadState = {
  filename: null,
  fileType: null,
  fileValid: false,
};

export const useUploadStore = create<UploadStore>()(
  subscribeWithSelector(
    persist(
      (set) => ({
        ...initialState,

        setUploadedFile: ({ filename, fileType, fileValid }: UploadedFile) =>
          set({ filename, fileType, fileValid }),

        clearUpload: () => set(initialState),
      }),
      {
        name: "hf-upload",
        storage: createJSONStorage(() => sessionStorage),
        partialize: (state) => ({
          filename: state.filename,
          fileType: state.fileType,
          fileValid: state.fileValid,
        }),
      }
    )
  )
);

```

```typescript
import { create } from "zustand";
import { subscribeWithSelector } from "zustand/middleware";
import { persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";

export type VoiceId = "soprano" | "alto" | "tenor" | "bass";

export const VOICE_IDS: VoiceId[] = ["soprano", "alto", "tenor", "bass"];

export interface EnsembleState {
  voices: Record<VoiceId, string | null>;
}

export interface EnsembleActions {
  setVoice: (voiceId: VoiceId, instrument: string | null) => void;
  clearEnsemble: () => void;
}

export type EnsembleStore = EnsembleState & EnsembleActions;

const initialState: EnsembleState = {
  voices: {
    soprano: null,
    alto: null,
    tenor: null,
    bass: null,
  },
};

export const useEnsembleStore = create<EnsembleStore>()(
  subscribeWithSelector(
    persist(
      (set) => ({
        ...initialState,

        setVoice: (voiceId: VoiceId, instrument: string | null) =>
          set((state) => ({
            voices: { ...state.voices, [voiceId]: instrument },
          })),

        clearEnsemble: () => set(initialState),
      }),
      {
        name: "hf-ensemble",
        storage: createJSONStorage(() => sessionStorage),
        partialize: (state) => ({ voices: state.voices }),
      }
    )
  )
);

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/shivpat/harmony-forge-redesign/src/store/useEnsembleStore.ts
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→"use client";
     2→
     3→import React from "react";
     4→import { useTheme } from "next-themes";
     5→import { UploadPromptContent } from "@/components/molecules/UploadPromptContent";
     6→import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
     7→
     8→export interface DropzoneCopyProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
     9→  onFileDrop?: (files: FileList) => void;
    10→  /** Called when user clicks the dropzone to open a file picker */
    11→  onFileSelect?: (files: FileList) => void;
    12→}
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Exact layout replication of Node 80t2V central content.
    16→ * Combines the Canvas (ktaiB) and Stand (n4ubU) into a single scalable ratio wrapper.
    17→ * Theme-aware: applies dark mode SVG colors from design system Nocturne tokens (Node PnDXj).
    18→ */
    19→export const DropzoneCopy = React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, DropzoneCopyProps>(
    20→  ({ className, onFileDrop, onFileSelect, ...props }, ref) => {
    21→    const [isHovered, setIsHovered] = React.useState(false);
    22→    const inputRef = React.useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null);
    23→    const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme();
    24→    // Render light-mode defaults on SSR and the first client paint to prevent hydration mismatch.
    25→    // After mount, resolvedTheme is read from localStorage and drives dark colors.
    26→    const [mounted, setMounted] = React.useState(false);
    27→    React.useEffect(() => {
    28→      setMounted(true);
    29→    }, []);
    30→    const isDark = mounted && resolvedTheme === "dark";
    31→
    32→    const handleDragOver = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
    33→      e.preventDefault();
    34→      setIsHovered(true);
    35→    };
    36→
    37→    const handleDragLeave = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
    38→      e.preventDefault();
    39→      setIsHovered(false);
    40→    };
    41→
    42→    const handleDrop = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
    43→      e.preventDefault();
    44→      setIsHovered(false);
    45→      if (e.dataTransfer.files && onFileDrop) {
    46→        onFileDrop(e.dataTransfer.files);
    47→      }
    48→    };
    49→
    50→    const handleClick = () => {
    51→      inputRef.current?.click();
    52→    };
    53→
    54→    const handleInputChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
    55→      if (e.target.files && e.target.files.length > 0) {
    56→        onFileSelect
    57→          ? onFileSelect(e.target.files)
    58→          : onFileDrop?.(e.target.files);
    59→      }
    60→    };
    61→
    62→    // Nocturne (Dark) — from globals.css & Node PnDXj
    63→    // Light: ktaiB_grad -> #fdf5e6 to #d2b48c; base #dec7a7; stem #d2b48c
    64→    // Dark:  ktaiB_grad -> #2d1817 to #3e2723; base #492c26; stem #3e2723
    65→    const gradStart = isDark ? "#2d1817" : "#fdf5e6";
    66→    const gradEnd = isDark ? "#3e2723" : "#d2b48c";
    67→    const baseColor = isDark ? "#492c26" : "#dec7a7";
    68→    const stemColor = isDark ? "#3e2723" : "#d2b48c";
    69→    const strokeColor = isDark
    70→      ? isHovered
    71→        ? "#f8f8f8"
    72→        : "#c8b8b6"
    73→      : isHovered
    74→        ? "#2d1817"
    75→        : "#7a6b69";
    76→
    77→    return (
    78→      <>
    79→        <input
    80→          ref={inputRef}
    81→          type="file"
    82→          accept=".pdf,.xml,.mxl,.midi,.mid"
    83→          className="sr-only"
    84→          aria-hidden="true"
    85→          tabIndex={-1}
    86→          onChange={handleInputChange}
    87→        />
    88→        <div
    89→          ref={ref}
    90→          className={cn(
    91→            "relative aspect-1513/880 mx-auto shrink-0 cursor-pointer",
    92→            className,
    93→          )}
    94→          onClick={handleClick}
    95→          onDragOver={handleDragOver}
    96→          onDragLeave={handleDragLeave}
    97→          onDrop={handleDrop}
    98→          role="button"
    99→          tabIndex={0}
   100→          aria-label="Upload score — click or drag a PDF, XML, or MIDI file"
   101→          onKeyDown={(e) => {
   102→            if (e.key === "Enter" || e.key === " ") {
   103→              e.preventDefault();
   104→              handleClick();
   105→            }
   106→          }}
   107→          {...props}
   108→        >
   109→          {/* ─── Unified SVG ─── viewBox maps exactly to design proportions ─── */}
   110→          <svg
   111→            className="absolute inset-0 w-full h-full drop-shadow-sm pointer-events-none"
   112→            viewBox="0 0 1513 1010"
   113→            fill="none"
   114→            xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
   115→            preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMax meet"
   116→          >
   117→            <defs>
   118→              {/* Gradient for the canvas panel (ktaiB / Rectangle 1) */}
   119→              <linearGradient
   120→                id="ktaiB_grad"
   121→                x1="719.5"
   122→                y1="0"
   123→                x2="719.5"
   124→                y2="648"
   125→                gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse"
   126→              >
   127→                <stop stopColor={gradStart} />
   128→                <stop offset="1" stopColor={gradEnd} />
   129→              </linearGradient>
   130→            </defs>
   131→
   132→            {/* ── 1. Wooden post / stem (1ZRgJ) — behind everything ───────────── */}
   133→            {/* Design: x:715.5 y:69 (in n4ubU), w:82 h:288. n4ubU starts at y:648. */}
   134→            {/* SVG y = 648 + 69 = 717. Visible to bottom of viewBox (1010 - 717 = 293px) */}
   135→            <rect x="715.5" y="717" width="82" height="293" fill={stemColor} />
   136→
   137→            {/* ── 2. Canvas panel (ktaiB / Frame 22) — main music stand body ─── */}
   138→            {/* Node ktaiB: Canvas trapezoid (1439px wide, 37px offset inside 1513px) */}
   139→            <g transform="translate(37, 0)">
   140→              {/* Backing plate trapezoid */}
   141→              <path

   143→                fill="url(#ktaiB_grad)"

   145→
   146→              {/* ── 3. Dashed drop-zone rect (SQz0K) ─────────────────────────── */}

**TASK-A05 preview** — inline error state for unsupported file types in `DropzoneCopy`.

```xml
<context>
  DropzoneCopy accepts drag-drop from the OS, which bypasses the input's
  accept attribute. A user can drop an .mp3 or .docx and the current code
  will silently proceed. The store now has fileValid, but the dropzone itself
  shows no feedback. POUR requires the error be announced via aria-live.
</context>
<task>
  Modify DropzoneCopy.tsx — when a drop or file-select produces a file with
  an extension not in the allowed list, render an inline error message inside
  the foreignObject area and announce it via aria-live="polite". Clear the
  error when a valid file is dropped or the component unmounts.
  Error text: "Unsupported file type. Please upload MusicXML, MIDI, or PDF."
</task>
<constraints>
  - Error color must use a CSS token, not a raw hex — read globals.css first
    to confirm the correct violation/error token name
  - aria-live="polite" on the error container (not assertive — not a blocking error)
  - Error state is local to DropzoneCopy (useState) — does not reach the store
  - Validation logic: same allowed extension set as page.tsx
  - The error clears when a valid file is subsequently dropped/selected
  - onFileDrop / onFileSelect callbacks are only called for valid files
</constraints>

```tsx
"use client";

import React from "react";
import { useTheme } from "next-themes";
import { UploadPromptContent } from "@/components/molecules/UploadPromptContent";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

export interface DropzoneCopyProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
  onFileDrop?: (files: FileList) => void;
  /** Called when user clicks the dropzone to open a file picker */
  onFileSelect?: (files: FileList) => void;
}

const ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = new Set([".pdf", ".xml", ".mxl", ".midi", ".mid"]);

function getExtension(filename: string): string {
  return "." + filename.split(".").pop()?.toLowerCase();
}

function isValidFile(file: File): boolean {
  return ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS.has(getExtension(file.name));
}

/**
 * Exact layout replication of Node 80t2V central content.
 * Combines the Canvas (ktaiB) and Stand (n4ubU) into a single scalable ratio wrapper.
 * Theme-aware: applies dark mode SVG colors from design system Nocturne tokens (Node PnDXj).
 */
export const DropzoneCopy = React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, DropzoneCopyProps>(
  ({ className, onFileDrop, onFileSelect, ...props }, ref) => {
    const [isHovered, setIsHovered] = React.useState(false);
    const [errorMessage, setErrorMessage] = React.useState<string | null>(null);
    const inputRef = React.useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null);
    const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme();
    // Render light-mode defaults on SSR and the first client paint to prevent hydration mismatch.
    // After mount, resolvedTheme is read from localStorage and drives dark colors.
    const [mounted, setMounted] = React.useState(false);
    React.useEffect(() => {
      setMounted(true);
    }, []);
    const isDark = mounted && resolvedTheme === "dark";

    const handleDragOver = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
      e.preventDefault();
      setIsHovered(true);
    };

    const handleDragLeave = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
      e.preventDefault();
      setIsHovered(false);
    };

    const handleDrop = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
      e.preventDefault();
      setIsHovered(false);
      if (!e.dataTransfer.files || e.dataTransfer.files.length === 0) return;
      const file = e.dataTransfer.files[0];
      if (!isValidFile(file)) {
        setErrorMessage(
          "Unsupported file type. Please upload MusicXML, MIDI, or PDF."
        );
        return;
      }
      setErrorMessage(null);
      onFileDrop?.(e.dataTransfer.files);
    };

    const handleClick = () => {
      inputRef.current?.click();
    };

    const handleInputChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
      if (!e.target.files || e.target.files.length === 0) return;
      const file = e.target.files[0];
      if (!isValidFile(file)) {
        setErrorMessage(
          "Unsupported file type. Please upload MusicXML, MIDI, or PDF."
        );
        return;
      }
      setErrorMessage(null);
      onFileSelect
        ? onFileSelect(e.target.files)
        : onFileDrop?.(e.target.files);
    };

    // Nocturne (Dark) — from globals.css & Node PnDXj
    // Light: ktaiB_grad -> #fdf5e6 to #d2b48c; base #dec7a7; stem #d2b48c
    // Dark:  ktaiB_grad -> #2d1817 to #3e2723; base #492c26; stem #3e2723
    const gradStart = isDark ? "#2d1817" : "#fdf5e6";
    const gradEnd = isDark ? "#3e2723" : "#d2b48c";
    const baseColor = isDark ? "#492c26" : "#dec7a7";
    const stemColor = isDark ? "#3e2723" : "#d2b48c";
    const strokeColor = isDark
      ? isHovered
        ? "#f8f8f8"
        : "#c8b8b6"
      : isHovered
        ? "#2d1817"
        : "#7a6b69";

    return (
      <>
        <input
          ref={inputRef}
          type="file"
          accept=".pdf,.xml,.mxl,.midi,.mid"
          className="sr-only"
          aria-hidden="true"
          tabIndex={-1}
          onChange={handleInputChange}
        />
        <div
          ref={ref}
          className={cn(
            "relative aspect-1513/880 mx-auto shrink-0 cursor-pointer",
            className,
          )}
          onClick={handleClick}
          onDragOver={handleDragOver}
          onDragLeave={handleDragLeave}
          onDrop={handleDrop}
          role="button"
          tabIndex={0}
          aria-label="Upload score — click or drag a PDF, XML, or MIDI file"
          onKeyDown={(e) => {
            if (e.key === "Enter" || e.key === " ") {
              e.preventDefault();
              handleClick();
            }
          }}
          {...props}
        >
          {/* ─── Unified SVG ─── viewBox maps exactly to design proportions ─── */}
          <svg
            className="absolute inset-0 w-full h-full drop-shadow-sm pointer-events-none"
            viewBox="0 0 1513 1010"
            fill="none"
            xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
            preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMax meet"
          >
            <defs>
              {/* Gradient for the canvas panel (ktaiB / Rectangle 1) */}
              <linearGradient
                id="ktaiB_grad"
                x1="719.5"
                y1="0"
                x2="719.5"
                y2="648"
                gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse"
              >
                <stop stopColor={gradStart} />
                <stop offset="1" stopColor={gradEnd} />
              </linearGradient>
            </defs>

            {/* ── 1. Wooden post / stem (1ZRgJ) — behind everything ───────────── */}
            {/* Design: x:715.5 y:69 (in n4ubU), w:82 h:288. n4ubU starts at y:648. */}
            {/* SVG y = 648 + 69 = 717. Visible to bottom of viewBox (1010 - 717 = 293px) */}
            <rect x="715.5" y="717" width="82" height="293" fill={stemColor} />

            {/* ── 2. Canvas panel (ktaiB / Frame 22) — main music stand body ─── */}
            {/* Node ktaiB: Canvas trapezoid (1439px wide, 37px offset inside 1513px) */}
            <g transform="translate(37, 0)">
              {/* Backing plate trapezoid */}
              <path
                d="M122 0l1195 0c12 0 24.2 7 25.1 19.8l96.9 628.2-1439 0 98.9-628.2c0.9-12.8 11.1-19.8 23.1-19.8z"
                fill="url(#ktaiB_grad)"
              />

              {/* ── 3. Dashed drop-zone rect (SQz0K) ─────────────────────────── */}
              {/* Design: x:482.5 y:41 w:474 h:566 */}
              <rect
                x="482.5"
                y="41"
                width="474"
                height="566"
                rx="20"
                stroke={errorMessage ? "var(--color-hf-violation)" : strokeColor}
                strokeOpacity={isHovered || errorMessage ? "1" : "0.5"}
                strokeWidth="2"
                strokeDasharray="10 5"
                className="transition-colors duration-300"
              />

              {/* ── 4. Upload prompt (zuzV6) ─────────────────────────────────── */}
              {/* Design: Frame 4 at x:560 y:214, we position the foreignObject exactly over the drop-zone rect */}
              <foreignObject x="482.5" y="41" width="474" height="566">
                <div className="w-full h-full flex flex-col items-center justify-center pointer-events-auto gap-4 px-8">
                  <UploadPromptContent isDark={isDark} />
                  {/* Error message — aria-live announces to screen readers on change */}
                  <div
                    aria-live="polite"
                    role="status"
                    className="text-center text-xs font-medium leading-snug"
                    style={{
                      color: errorMessage
                        ? "var(--color-hf-violation)"
                        : "transparent",
                      minHeight: "2.5rem",
                    }}
                  >
                    {errorMessage ?? ""}
                  </div>
                </div>
              </foreignObject>
            </g>

            {/* ── 5. Shelf base (fqxzb) ────────────────────────────────────── */}
            {/* Design: fill:#dec7a7, h:70, w:1513, cornerRadius:20 (all corners) */}
            <rect
              x="0"
              y="648"
              width="1513"
              height="70"
              rx="20"
              fill={baseColor}
            />
          </svg>
        </div>
      </>
    );
  },
);

DropzoneCopy.displayName = "DropzoneCopy";

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-10 17:23:40Z)**_

I see a shape mismatch before writing anything. Here's the preview with the issue surfaced.

```xml
<context>
  EnsembleBuilderPanel uses Record<VoiceType, string[]> — each voice has an
  array of selected instruments (multi-select). useEnsembleStore (A02) was
  designed as Record<VoiceId, string | null> — single instrument per slot.
  These two shapes are incompatible. Wiring them without resolving the
  mismatch would either break the UI or silently discard multi-selections.
</context>
<task>
  Resolve the shape mismatch, then wire EnsembleBuilderPanel to the store
  so selections survive navigation to /sandbox.
</task>
<constraints>
  - SATB voice-leading allows doubling (multiple instruments per part in
    orchestral arrangements) — multi-select is architecturally valid
  - No prop-drilling; useEnsembleStore imported directly in organism
  - subscribeWithSelector + persist (sessionStorage) must survive
  - No any types
</constraints>
<output>
  Updated useEnsembleStore.ts (shape change) + modified EnsembleBuilderPanel.tsx
</output>
```

**Shape mismatch — decision required:**

| Option | Store shape | Component change | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** — Widen store to `string[]` | `Record<VoiceId, string[]>` | None — matches current UI | Store shape grows; ADR-008 updates |
| **B** — Narrow component to single-select | `Record<VoiceId, string \| null>` | Remove multi-select from VoiceDropdown | UI regression; user loses multi-instrument feature |

Option A is correct. The component's multi-select is an intentional design feature (multiple instruments per SATB voice is valid in orchestral SATB writing). The plan's `string | null` shape was an underspecification.

**Proposed:** Update `useEnsembleStore` to `Record<VoiceId, string[]>`, replace `setVoice` with `toggleVoiceInstrument(voiceId, instrument)` + `clearEnsemble`, then wire the panel.

> **Shall I proceed with Option A (widen store shape to string[]) as described above? (Yes / No / Request changes)**

# 🐝 Claude-Flow Swarm Coordination

Advanced multi-agent coordination system with timeout-free execution, distributed memory sharing, and intelligent load balancing.

## Basic Usage
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "your complex task" --strategy <type> [options]
```

## 🎯 Swarm Strategies
- **auto** - Automatic strategy selection based on task analysis
- **development** - Code implementation with review and testing
- **research** - Information gathering and synthesis
- **analysis** - Data processing and pattern identification
- **testing** - Comprehensive quality assurance
- **optimization** - Performance tuning and refactoring
- **maintenance** - System updates and bug fixes

## 🤖 Agent Types
- **coordinator** - Plans and delegates tasks to other agents
- **developer** - Writes code and implements solutions
- **researcher** - Gathers and analyzes information
- **analyzer** - Identifies patterns and generates insights
- **tester** - Creates and runs tests for quality assurance
- **reviewer** - Performs code and design reviews
- **documenter** - Creates documentation and guides
- **monitor** - Tracks performance and system health
- **specialist** - Domain-specific expert agents

## 🔄 Coordination Modes
- **centralized** - Single coordinator manages all agents (default)
- **distributed** - Multiple coordinators share management
- **hierarchical** - Tree structure with nested coordination
- **mesh** - Peer-to-peer agent collaboration
- **hybrid** - Mixed coordination strategies

## ⚙️ Common Options
- `--strategy <type>` - Execution strategy
- `--mode <type>` - Coordination mode
- `--max-agents <n>` - Maximum concurrent agents (default: 5)
- `--timeout <minutes>` - Timeout in minutes (default: 60)
- `--background` - Run in background for tasks > 30 minutes
- `--monitor` - Enable real-time monitoring
- `--ui` - Launch terminal UI interface
- `--parallel` - Enable parallel execution
- `--distributed` - Enable distributed coordination
- `--review` - Enable peer review process
- `--testing` - Include automated testing
- `--encryption` - Enable data encryption
- `--verbose` - Detailed logging output
- `--dry-run` - Show configuration without executing

## 🌟 Examples

### Development Swarm with Review
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Build e-commerce REST API" \
  --strategy development \
  --monitor \
  --review \
  --testing
```

### Long-Running Research Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Analyze AI market trends 2024-2025" \
  --strategy research \
  --background \
  --distributed \
  --max-agents 8
```

### Performance Optimization Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Optimize database queries and API performance" \
  --strategy optimization \
  --testing \
  --parallel \
  --monitor
```

### Enterprise Development Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Implement secure payment processing system" \
  --strategy development \
  --mode distributed \
  --max-agents 10 \
  --parallel \
  --monitor \
  --review \
  --testing \
  --encryption \
  --verbose
```

### Testing and QA Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Comprehensive security audit and testing" \
  --strategy testing \
  --review \
  --verbose \
  --max-agents 6
```

## 📊 Monitoring and Control

### Real-time monitoring:
```bash
# Monitor swarm activity
./claude-flow monitor

# Monitor specific component
./claude-flow monitor --focus swarm
```

### Check swarm status:
```bash
# Overall system status
./claude-flow status

# Detailed swarm status
./claude-flow status --verbose
```

### View agent activity:
```bash
# List all agents
./claude-flow agent list

# Agent details
./claude-flow agent info <agent-id>
```

## 💾 Memory Integration

Swarms automatically use distributed memory for collaboration:

```bash
# Store swarm objectives
./claude-flow memory store "swarm_objective" "Build scalable API" --namespace swarm

# Query swarm progress
./claude-flow memory query "swarm_progress" --namespace swarm

# Export swarm memory

# 🐝 Claude-Flow Swarm Coordination

Advanced multi-agent coordination system with timeout-free execution, distributed memory sharing, and intelligent load balancing.

## Basic Usage
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "your complex task" --strategy <type> [options]
```

## 🎯 Swarm Strategies
- **auto** - Automatic strategy selection based on task analysis
- **development** - Code implementation with review and testing
- **research** - Information gathering and synthesis
- **analysis** - Data processing and pattern identification
- **testing** - Comprehensive quality assurance
- **optimization** - Performance tuning and refactoring
- **maintenance** - System updates and bug fixes

## 🤖 Agent Types
- **coordinator** - Plans and delegates tasks to other agents
- **developer** - Writes code and implements solutions
- **researcher** - Gathers and analyzes information
- **analyzer** - Identifies patterns and generates insights
- **tester** - Creates and runs tests for quality assurance
- **reviewer** - Performs code and design reviews
- **documenter** - Creates documentation and guides
- **monitor** - Tracks performance and system health
- **specialist** - Domain-specific expert agents

## 🔄 Coordination Modes
- **centralized** - Single coordinator manages all agents (default)
- **distributed** - Multiple coordinators share management
- **hierarchical** - Tree structure with nested coordination
- **mesh** - Peer-to-peer agent collaboration
- **hybrid** - Mixed coordination strategies

## ⚙️ Common Options
- `--strategy <type>` - Execution strategy
- `--mode <type>` - Coordination mode
- `--max-agents <n>` - Maximum concurrent agents (default: 5)
- `--timeout <minutes>` - Timeout in minutes (default: 60)
- `--background` - Run in background for tasks > 30 minutes
- `--monitor` - Enable real-time monitoring
- `--ui` - Launch terminal UI interface
- `--parallel` - Enable parallel execution
- `--distributed` - Enable distributed coordination
- `--review` - Enable peer review process
- `--testing` - Include automated testing
- `--encryption` - Enable data encryption
- `--verbose` - Detailed logging output
- `--dry-run` - Show configuration without executing

## 🌟 Examples

### Development Swarm with Review
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Build e-commerce REST API" \
  --strategy development \
  --monitor \
  --review \
  --testing
```

### Long-Running Research Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Analyze AI market trends 2024-2025" \
  --strategy research \
  --background \
  --distributed \
  --max-agents 8
```

### Performance Optimization Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Optimize database queries and API performance" \
  --strategy optimization \
  --testing \
  --parallel \
  --monitor
```

### Enterprise Development Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Implement secure payment processing system" \
  --strategy development \
  --mode distributed \
  --max-agents 10 \
  --parallel \
  --monitor \
  --review \
  --testing \
  --encryption \
  --verbose
```

### Testing and QA Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Comprehensive security audit and testing" \
  --strategy testing \
  --review \
  --verbose \
  --max-agents 6
```

## 📊 Monitoring and Control

### Real-time monitoring:
```bash
# Monitor swarm activity
./claude-flow monitor

# Monitor specific component
./claude-flow monitor --focus swarm
```

### Check swarm status:
```bash
# Overall system status
./claude-flow status

# Detailed swarm status
./claude-flow status --verbose
```

### View agent activity:
```bash
# List all agents
./claude-flow agent list

# Agent details
./claude-flow agent info <agent-id>
```

## 💾 Memory Integration

Swarms automatically use distributed memory for collaboration:

```bash
# Store swarm objectives
./claude-flow memory store "swarm_objective" "Build scalable API" --namespace swarm

# Query swarm progress
./claude-flow memory query "swarm_progress" --namespace swarm

# Export swarm memory
./claude-flow memory export swarm-results.json --namespace swarm
```

## 🎯 Key Features

### Timeout-Free Execution
- Background mode for long-running tasks
- State persistence across sessions
- Automatic checkpoint recovery

### Work Stealing & Load Balancing
- Dynamic task redistribution
- Automatic agent scaling
- Resource-aware scheduling

### Circuit Breakers & Fault Tolerance
- Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- Graceful degradation
- Health monitoring and recovery

### Real-Time Collaboration
- Cross-agent communication
- Shared memory access
- Event-driven coordination

### Enterprise Security
- Role-based access control
- Audit logging
- Data encryption
- Input validation

## 🔧 Advanced Configuration

### Dry run to preview:
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Test task" --dry-run --strategy development
```

### Custom quality thresholds:
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "High quality API" \
  --strategy development \
  --quality-threshold 0.95
```

### Scheduling algorithms:

```typescript
import { create } from "zustand";
import { subscribeWithSelector } from "zustand/middleware";
import { persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";

export type VoiceId = "soprano" | "alto" | "tenor" | "bass";

export const VOICE_IDS: VoiceId[] = ["soprano", "alto", "tenor", "bass"];

export interface EnsembleState {
  voices: Record<VoiceId, string[]>;
}

export interface EnsembleActions {
  toggleVoiceInstrument: (voiceId: VoiceId, instrument: string) => void;
  clearEnsemble: () => void;
}

export type EnsembleStore = EnsembleState & EnsembleActions;

const initialState: EnsembleState = {
  voices: { soprano: [], alto: [], tenor: [], bass: [] },
};

export const useEnsembleStore = create<EnsembleStore>()(
  subscribeWithSelector(
    persist(
      (set) => ({
        ...initialState,

        toggleVoiceInstrument: (voiceId: VoiceId, instrument: string) =>
          set((state) => {
            const current = state.voices[voiceId];
            const updated = current.includes(instrument)
              ? current.filter((i) => i !== instrument)
              : [...current, instrument];
            return { voices: { ...state.voices, [voiceId]: updated } };
          }),

        clearEnsemble: () => set(initialState),
      }),
      {
        name: "hf-ensemble",
        storage: createJSONStorage(() => sessionStorage),
        partialize: (state) => ({ voices: state.voices }),
      }
    )
  )
);

```

The file /Users/shivpat/harmony-forge-redesign/src/store/useEnsembleStore.ts has been updated successfully.
```

"use client";

import React from "react";
import { Sparkles } from "lucide-react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
import { VoiceDropdown } from "@/components/molecules/VoiceDropdown";
import { EnsemblePreviewCard, type SelectedPart } from "@/components/molecules/EnsemblePreviewCard";
import type { VoiceType } from "@/components/atoms/PartChip";
import { useEnsembleStore } from "@/store/useEnsembleStore";

/** Placeholder instrument lists per voice — replaced by backend data later */
const VOICE_INSTRUMENTS: Record<VoiceType, string[]> = {
  soprano: ["Soprano Voice", "Flute", "Oboe", "Violin I"],
  alto: ["Alto Voice", "Clarinet", "Viola", "French Horn"],
  tenor: ["Tenor Voice", "Trumpet", "Cello", "Trombone"],
  bass: ["Bass Voice", "Bassoon", "Double Bass", "Tuba"],
};

const VOICE_ORDER: VoiceType[] = ["soprano", "alto", "tenor", "bass"];

export interface EnsembleBuilderPanelProps
  extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
  onGenerateHarmonies?: () => void;
}

/**
 * EnsembleBuilderPanel Organism
 * Extracted from Pencil Node ID: ZlAUA ("Ensemble Builder Panel")
 * Right column: heading → 4 VoiceDropdowns → divider → EnsemblePreviewCard → Generate CTA.
 * Reads/writes voice selections via useEnsembleStore. Client component.
 */
export const EnsembleBuilderPanel = React.forwardRef<
  HTMLDivElement,
  EnsembleBuilderPanelProps
>(({ onGenerateHarmonies, className, ...props }, ref) => {
  const voices = useEnsembleStore((s) => s.voices);
  const toggleVoiceInstrument = useEnsembleStore((s) => s.toggleVoiceInstrument);

  const handleToggle = (voice: VoiceType, instrument: string) => {
    toggleVoiceInstrument(voice, instrument);
  };

  const handleRemovePart = (label: string) => {
    for (const voice of VOICE_ORDER) {
      if (voices[voice].includes(label)) {
        toggleVoiceInstrument(voice, label);
      }
    }
  };

  // Flatten all selected parts into SelectedPart[] for preview card
  const selectedParts: SelectedPart[] = VOICE_ORDER.flatMap((voice) =>
    voices[voice].map((label) => ({ label, voice })),
  );

  return (
    <div
      ref={ref}
      className={cn(
        "flex flex-col gap-[24px] flex-1 h-full overflow-y-auto",
        "px-[48px] pt-[32px] pb-[32px]",
        className,
      )}
      style={{
        backgroundColor: "var(--hf-panel-bg)",
        borderLeft: "1px solid var(--hf-detail)",
      }}
      {...props}
    >
      {/* Heading — Node eOoBk */}
      <div className="flex flex-col gap-[6px]">
        <h2
          className="font-brand text-[26px] font-normal leading-none"
          style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-primary)" }}
        >
          Ensemble Builder
        </h2>
        <p
          className="font-mono text-[12px] font-normal leading-none"
          style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-secondary)" }}
        >
          Select instruments for your arrangement
        </p>
      </div>

      {/* Voice List — Node iVLue */}
      <div className="flex flex-col gap-[10px] w-full">
        {VOICE_ORDER.map((voice) => (
          <VoiceDropdown
            key={voice}
            voice={voice}
            instruments={VOICE_INSTRUMENTS[voice]}
            selected={voices[voice]}
            onToggle={(instrument) => handleToggle(voice, instrument)}
          />
        ))}
      </div>

      {/* Divider — Node IPbv5 */}
      <div
        className="w-full h-[1px] shrink-0"
        style={{
          backgroundColor: "var(--hf-detail)",
          opacity: 0.4,
        }}
        aria-hidden="true"
      />

      {/* Ensemble Preview Card — Node LtBtZ */}
      <EnsemblePreviewCard
        selectedParts={selectedParts}
        totalParts={12}
        onRemovePart={handleRemovePart}
      />

      {/* Generate CTA row — Node nrVwz / rJKG6 */}
      <div className="flex justify-end w-full">
        <button
          type="button"
          onClick={onGenerateHarmonies}
          className={cn(
            "flex items-center gap-[8px] rounded-[6px] px-[24px] py-[12px]",
            "font-mono text-[13px] font-bold leading-none",
            "transition-opacity duration-150 hover:opacity-90 active:opacity-80",
            "focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-[var(--hf-surface)]",
          )}
          style={{
            backgroundColor: "var(--hf-accent)",
            color: "var(--text-on-light)",
          }}
          aria-label="Generate harmonies"
        >
          <Sparkles
            className="w-4 h-4 shrink-0"
            aria-hidden="true"
          />
          Generate Harmonies
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
});

EnsembleBuilderPanel.displayName = "EnsembleBuilderPanel";

```

```tsx
"use client";

import React from "react";
import { Sparkles } from "lucide-react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
import { VoiceDropdown } from "@/components/molecules/VoiceDropdown";
import { EnsemblePreviewCard, type SelectedPart } from "@/components/molecules/EnsemblePreviewCard";
import type { VoiceType } from "@/components/atoms/PartChip";
import { useEnsembleStore } from "@/store/useEnsembleStore";
import type { VoiceId } from "@/store/useEnsembleStore";

/** Placeholder instrument lists per voice — replaced by backend data later */
const VOICE_INSTRUMENTS: Record<VoiceType, string[]> = {
  soprano: ["Soprano Voice", "Flute", "Oboe", "Violin I"],
  alto: ["Alto Voice", "Clarinet", "Viola", "French Horn"],
  tenor: ["Tenor Voice", "Trumpet", "Cello", "Trombone"],
  bass: ["Bass Voice", "Bassoon", "Double Bass", "Tuba"],
};

const VOICE_ORDER: VoiceType[] = ["soprano", "alto", "tenor", "bass"];

export interface EnsembleBuilderPanelProps
  extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
  onGenerateHarmonies?: () => void;
}

/**
 * EnsembleBuilderPanel Organism
 * Extracted from Pencil Node ID: ZlAUA ("Ensemble Builder Panel")
 * Right column: heading → 4 VoiceDropdowns → divider → EnsemblePreviewCard → Generate CTA.
 * Reads/writes voice selections from useEnsembleStore (persisted to sessionStorage).
 */
export const EnsembleBuilderPanel = React.forwardRef<
  HTMLDivElement,
  EnsembleBuilderPanelProps
>(({ onGenerateHarmonies, className, ...props }, ref) => {
  const voices = useEnsembleStore((s) => s.voices);
  const toggleVoiceInstrument = useEnsembleStore((s) => s.toggleVoiceInstrument);

  const handleToggle = (voice: VoiceId, instrument: string) => {
    toggleVoiceInstrument(voice, instrument);
  };

  const handleRemovePart = (label: string) => {
    for (const voice of VOICE_ORDER) {
      if (voices[voice].includes(label)) {
        toggleVoiceInstrument(voice, label);
        break;
      }
    }
  };

  // Flatten all selected parts into SelectedPart[] for preview card
  const selectedParts: SelectedPart[] = VOICE_ORDER.flatMap((voice) =>
    voices[voice].map((label) => ({ label, voice })),
  );

  return (
    <div
      ref={ref}
      className={cn(
        "flex flex-col gap-[24px] flex-1 h-full overflow-y-auto",
        "px-[48px] pt-[32px] pb-[32px]",
        className,
      )}
      style={{
        backgroundColor: "var(--hf-panel-bg)",
        borderLeft: "1px solid var(--hf-detail)",
      }}
      {...props}
    >
      {/* Heading — Node eOoBk */}
      <div className="flex flex-col gap-[6px]">
        <h2
          className="font-brand text-[26px] font-normal leading-none"
          style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-primary)" }}
        >
          Ensemble Builder
        </h2>
        <p
          className="font-mono text-[12px] font-normal leading-none"
          style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-secondary)" }}
        >
          Select instruments for your arrangement
        </p>
      </div>

      {/* Voice List — Node iVLue */}
      <div className="flex flex-col gap-[10px] w-full">
        {VOICE_ORDER.map((voice) => (
          <VoiceDropdown
            key={voice}
            voice={voice}
            instruments={VOICE_INSTRUMENTS[voice]}
            selected={voices[voice]}
            onToggle={(instrument) => handleToggle(voice, instrument)}
          />
        ))}
      </div>

      {/* Divider — Node IPbv5 */}
      <div
        className="w-full h-[1px] shrink-0"
        style={{
          backgroundColor: "var(--hf-detail)",
          opacity: 0.4,
        }}
        aria-hidden="true"
      />

      {/* Ensemble Preview Card — Node LtBtZ */}
      <EnsemblePreviewCard
        selectedParts={selectedParts}
        totalParts={12}
        onRemovePart={handleRemovePart}
      />

      {/* Generate CTA row — Node nrVwz / rJKG6 */}
      <div className="flex justify-end w-full">
        <button
          type="button"
          onClick={onGenerateHarmonies}
          className={cn(
            "flex items-center gap-[8px] rounded-[6px] px-[24px] py-[12px]",
            "font-mono text-[13px] font-bold leading-none",
            "transition-opacity duration-150 hover:opacity-90 active:opacity-80",
            "focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-[var(--hf-surface)]",
          )}
          style={{
            backgroundColor: "var(--hf-accent)",
            color: "var(--text-on-light)",
          }}
          aria-label="Generate harmonies"
        >
          <Sparkles
            className="w-4 h-4 shrink-0"
            aria-hidden="true"
          />
          Generate Harmonies
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
});

EnsembleBuilderPanel.displayName = "EnsembleBuilderPanel";

```

    97→                  aria-hidden="true"

```xml
<context>
  The / route has no explicit <title>. Next.js App Router exposes the
  metadata API for this — a static export is all that's needed.
</context>
<task>
  Add `export const metadata` to src/app/page.tsx with
  title: "HarmonyForge — Upload Your Score".
</task>
<constraints>
  - Next.js App Router metadata API (not react-helmet, not <Head>)
  - page.tsx is a "use client" component — metadata must be exported
    from a Server Component. The metadata export cannot coexist with
    "use client" in the same file.
  - Correct approach: move the metadata export to src/app/layout.tsx
    as a page-specific override, OR create a thin server wrapper.
    The cleanest fix: add a generateMetadata or static metadata export
    to layout.tsx scoped to the / route — but layout.tsx applies to all
    routes, so per-route metadata needs to live in a separate server
    component layer above page.tsx.
  - Simplest valid approach: remove "use client" from page.tsx is not
    viable (it uses useRouter + useState). Instead, read layout.tsx first
    to check if per-route metadata is already handled there, then decide.
</constraints>
<output>
  Correct metadata placement confirmed after reading layout.tsx
</output>
```

```tsx
"use client";

import React from "react";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";
import { DocumentHeader } from "@/components/organisms/DocumentHeader";
import { PlaygroundBackground } from "@/components/organisms/PlaygroundBackground";
import { DropzoneCopy } from "@/components/organisms/DropzoneCopy";
import { TransitionOverlay } from "@/components/organisms/TransitionOverlay";
import { BrandTitle } from "@/components/atoms/BrandTitle";
import { useUploadStore } from "@/store/useUploadStore";

const ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = new Set([".pdf", ".xml", ".mxl", ".midi", ".mid"]);

/**
 * HomeView — client component for the / (Playground) route.
 * Extracted from page.tsx to allow page.tsx to be a Server Component
 * and export per-route Next.js metadata.
 */
export function HomeView() {
  const router = useRouter();
  const [isTransitioning, setIsTransitioning] = React.useState(false);
  const setUploadedFile = useUploadStore((state) => state.setUploadedFile);

  const handleFileUpload = (files: FileList) => {
    const file = files[0];
    if (!file) return;
    const ext = "." + file.name.split(".").pop()?.toLowerCase();
    const fileValid = ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS.has(ext);
    setUploadedFile({ filename: file.name, fileType: file.type, fileValid });
    setIsTransitioning(true);
    setTimeout(() => {
      router.push("/document");
    }, 2000);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <PlaygroundBackground className="flex flex-col">
        {/* Header with StepBar set to Step 1 (Playground) */}
        <DocumentHeader currentStep={1} />

        {/* Main Content: Brand Title and scaled-down Dropzone */}
        <main className="flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-between pb-0 overflow-y-auto">
          {/* Title takes up the top empty space and centers vertically before the stand */}
          <div className="flex-1 flex items-center justify-center pt-8">
            <BrandTitle className="text-center" />
          </div>

          {/* Scaled down music stand (max 1000px) flush with the bottom */}
          <div className="w-full max-w-[1000px] shrink-0 mt-8">
            <DropzoneCopy
              onFileDrop={handleFileUpload}
              onFileSelect={handleFileUpload}
              className="w-full"
            />
          </div>
        </main>
      </PlaygroundBackground>

      {/* Loading overlay — mounts over everything */}
      <TransitionOverlay variant="parsing" visible={isTransitioning} />
    </>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-10 19:40:02Z)**_

"Effort level" in Claude Code refers to the extended thinking budget — it's a runtime setting, not a config file option. I can't toggle it from within a session via file edits.

**To switch to medium effort:**

In your Claude Code terminal, run:
```
/model
```
This opens the model picker where you can select the thinking mode (None / Low / Medium / High). Select **Medium**.

Alternatively, if you're using the Claude Code desktop or VS Code extension, look for the model/thinking indicator in the bottom status bar and click it.

The `settings.json` in this repo controls hook behavior and claude-flow config — it doesn't expose the thinking budget.

Ready to continue with **TASK-A09** whenever you are, Superman.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-10 19:46:09Z)**_

```xml
<context>
  DocumentHeader shows on both / and /document. The spec requires a back
  button on the document page — but not on the landing page (there's nowhere
  to go back to from step 1). The back button must be conditional on the
  current route or passed as a prop.
</context>
<task>
  Add an optional showBack prop to DocumentHeader. When true, render a back
  button left of LogoLockup that calls router.push('/'). The button needs
  aria-label="Back to upload" and a token focus ring. DocumentHeader becomes
  a "use client" component only if showBack is used — use a pattern that
  avoids forcing the server component at / to lose its status.
</task>
<constraints>
  - DocumentHeader is currently a server-compatible component (no "use client")
  - Adding useRouter forces "use client" — acceptable here since DocumentHeader
    is already used inside HomeView (client) and document/page.tsx (client)
  - showBack?: boolean prop — false/undefined renders nothing in that slot
  - aria-label="Back to upload" verbatim (spec AC-001)
  - Focus ring: focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 with token color
  - No new file needed — modify DocumentHeader directly
</constraints>
<output>
  src/components/organisms/DocumentHeader.tsx — modified in place
</output>
```

**Meta-rewrite:** Add `"use client"`, `useRouter`, `showBack` prop, conditional back button. Minimum: ~10 lines added.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-10 19:46:29Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

import React from "react";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";
import { ArrowLeft } from "lucide-react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
import { LogoLockup } from "@/components/atoms/LogoLockup";
import { StepBar } from "@/components/molecules/StepBar";
import { ThemeToggle } from "@/components/atoms/ThemeToggle";

export interface DocumentHeaderProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement> {
  currentStep?: 1 | 2 | 3;
  /** Render a back-to-upload button left of the logo (document page only) */
  showBack?: boolean;
}

/**
 * DocumentHeader Organism
 * Extracted from Pencil Node ID: tHpLP ("Header")
 * 64px top bar: [Back?] Logo (left) · StepBar (center) · ThemeToggle (right).
 * Bottom border uses --hf-detail (light) / --nocturne-surface-40 (dark).
 */
export const DocumentHeader = React.forwardRef<
  HTMLElement,
  DocumentHeaderProps
>(({ currentStep = 2, showBack = false, className, ...props }, ref) => {
  const router = useRouter();

  return (
    <header
      ref={ref}
      className={cn(
        "flex items-center justify-between w-full h-[64px] shrink-0",
        "px-[40px]",
        className,
      )}
      style={{
        backgroundColor: "var(--hf-bg)",
        borderBottom: "1px solid var(--hf-detail)",
      }}
      {...props}
    >
      {/* Left: optional back button + Logo */}
      <div className="flex items-center gap-[12px]">
        {showBack && (
          <button
            type="button"
            onClick={() => router.push("/")}
            aria-label="Back to upload"
            className={cn(
              "flex items-center justify-center w-8 h-8 rounded-[6px]",
              "transition-opacity duration-150 hover:opacity-70",
              "focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2",
              "focus-visible:outline-[var(--hf-accent)]",
            )}
            style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-secondary)" }}
          >
            <ArrowLeft className="w-4 h-4" aria-hidden="true" />
          </button>
        )}
        <LogoLockup />
      </div>

      {/* Center: Progress steps */}
      <StepBar currentStep={currentStep} aria-label="Arrangement progress" />

      {/* Right: Theme toggle */}
      <ThemeToggle />
    </header>
  );
});

DocumentHeader.displayName = "DocumentHeader";

```

```tsx
"use client";

import React from "react";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";
import { DocumentHeader } from "@/components/organisms/DocumentHeader";
import { ScorePreviewPanel } from "@/components/organisms/ScorePreviewPanel";
import { EnsembleBuilderPanel } from "@/components/organisms/EnsembleBuilderPanel";
import { TransitionOverlay } from "@/components/organisms/TransitionOverlay";
import { useUploadStore } from "@/store/useUploadStore";

/**
 * Document Page — /document
 * Step 2: Score preview (left) + Ensemble Builder (right).
 * On "Generate Harmonies" → shows "Generating Harmonies" overlay → navigates to /sandbox.
 * Corresponds to Pencil Node ID: iNmP7 (Sonata) / lXHYH (Nocturne).
 */
export default function DocumentPage() {
  const router = useRouter();
  const [isTransitioning, setIsTransitioning] = React.useState(false);
  const filename = useUploadStore((s) => s.filename);

  // Redirect guard — if no file has been uploaded, send back to upload step
  React.useEffect(() => {
    if (!filename) {
      router.push("/");
    }
  }, [filename, router]);

  // Derive piece title: strip file extension from filename
  const pieceTitle = filename
    ? filename.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, "")
    : "Untitled Score";

  const handleGenerate = () => {
    setIsTransitioning(true);
    // Show "Generating Harmonies" overlay for 2.2s, then navigate
    setTimeout(() => {
      router.push("/sandbox");
    }, 2200);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <div className="flex flex-col w-screen h-screen overflow-hidden bg-(--hf-bg)">
        {/* Top navigation bar */}
        <DocumentHeader currentStep={2} showBack />

        {/* Two-panel body */}
        <div className="flex flex-row flex-1 min-h-0">
          <ScorePreviewPanel
            scoreTitle={pieceTitle}
            canvasAriaLabel={`Score preview: ${pieceTitle}`}
          />
          <EnsembleBuilderPanel onGenerateHarmonies={handleGenerate} />
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Loading overlay */}
      <TransitionOverlay variant="generating" visible={isTransitioning} />
    </>
  );
}

```
